  •  Remington M700
en.st_up_prih_b1_descr: An amortization spring recoil buffer partially removes the discomfort experienced when firing a hand-held machine gun.
en.st_up_zatp_c2_descr: Long bolt movement gives a bullet greater energy.
en.st_af_ah_h2_descr:
A lump of shriveled clay and organic debris. Separation of individual lumps of material seems impossible, due to the presence of some unknown and incredibly strong binding agent. The gravitational properties of the artefact seem to have effects similar to that of hemophilia.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Artefact
en.st_up_barp_a2_descr: Barrel perforation causes a reduction in recoil with gas escaping via special holes.
en.st_up_stvp_c1_descr: This modification is only possible if a return spring has been installed into the handle.
en.st_up_bard_c5_descr: When the weapon is fired, an active counter-weight simultaneously moves to the muzzle section of the barrel, thus compensating for part of the recoil.
en.st_up_stvd_b1_descr: Addition of a mass component to the weapon to reduce recoil.
en.st_up_autd_c5_descr: An automatic driving rod improves a weapon's precision.
en.st_up_usmp_c3_descr: Once the first shot is fired, half the trigger movement is required to make subsequent shots.
en.st_cigar2_descr:
A pack of aged cigarettes produced during the Soviet era, remarkably preserved and still carrying the distinct, harsh flavor reminiscent of the USSR’s golden years. Despite their age, the unique blend of low-grade tobacco and paper filters keeps the signature Soviet taste intact, evoking a sense of nostalgia among older inhabitants of the Zone.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Stimulant
  •  Alcohol Sobering Effect
en.st_up_stvd_c1_descr: A mercury recoil absorber is located in the weapon's stock, partially consuming some of the recoil when the weapon is fired.
en.st_up_autd_c2_descr: When a shot is fired, a component opens a path into the barrel and returns to the closed position by means of a lengthwise sliding movement of the foregrip.
en.st_up_stvs_b3_descr: Longitudinal fullers of the barrel section prevent the barrel from overheating and dirt contamination.
en.st_up_auts_a2_descr: This component allows the shooter to get a feel for the moment at which the shot is fired, improving firing accuracy.
en.st_up_1921_b5_descr: Installation of polymer stock for weight reduction.
en.st_up_1921_b6_descr: Installation of polymer handguard for weight reduction.
en.st_up_bard_c2_descr: The rifled part of the barrel makes it impossible to fire shot, but the accuracy achieved outweighs this drawback.
en.st_up_zatp_b3_descr: Sputter protects weapons from early wear.
en.st_wpn_aug_a1_bas_descr:
The AUG (Armee-Universal-Gewehr) is the standard small arm of many Armed Forces and Special Units all over the world since 1977. It is famous for its modular concept “Bullpup“ design making it a short, well-balanced rifle with high accuracy and reliability. The A1 variant is fitted with an integrated 1.5x scope.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  origin: Austria
  •  manufacturer: Steyr Arms
en.st_addon_sil_hexagon_descr:
A specialized suppressor for SKS rifles chambered in 7.62x39mm, offering effective sound reduction without compromising accuracy.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  7.62mm
en.st_meat_chimera_descr:
A rare and exotic dish made from Chimera meat, carefully cooked to break down its dense muscle fibers and served alongside mushrooms and various spices. An expensive meal, eaten most commonly by veteran stalkers to celebrate the accomplishment of difficult missions.
 
 NOTE: The quality and healthiness of a meal is determined by how it is prepared.
  •  Zone-produced meals, while less contaminated, are still unhealthy.
  •  Stewed meals require the addition of clean water and are significantly less contaminated.
  •  Purified meals required the addition of vodka and are not only radiation-free but provide revitalizing effects.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  High-tier Cooked Meal
en.st_up_chem_1_descr: Basic protection against rain and caustic substances.
en.st_ecologist_report_1_of_3_name: Walking Dead Findings Field Report 39B rev. 2 (Part 1 of 3)
en.st_gamma_recipe_basic_4: The Art of Artefacts Fusion Vol. 1
en.st_up_zatp_c6_descr: Modernization of the pistol's automatic parts suitable for automatic fire.
en.st_up_armor_3_descr: Armor with an inner layer that spreads impact over a greater area.
en.st_up_usm_b1_descr: A special selection of components made from various materials specific to that weapon. It may not be cheap, but it increases the life cycle of your firearm.
en.st_up_zatp_c5_descr: Introduction of additional compensation holes brings about a significant reduction in recoil.
en.st_addon_scope_1p29_descr:
The 1P29 (aka UPO-1 / USP-1) is a universal sight made for Soviet/Russian small arms. Introduced in the 1980s, it is a compact, rugged prismatic optic (inspired by the British SUIT/Trilux designs), often fitted with a tritium/ambient illumination element and a stadiametric range scale for quick distance estimation; it saw service with late-Cold War and post-Soviet forces.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Soviet Union/Russia
  •  Manufacturer: NPZ
  •  Magnification: 4x
en.st_addon_scope_1p59_descr:
A Russian variable-magnification sniper sight introduced around 2001 for modern SVD-class rifles. It features a crisp, thin reticle, bright image, and battery powered illumination.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: KMZ
  •  Magnification: 3–10x
  •  Reticle illumination: press Mark Switch key
en.st_af_star_phantom_descr:
A purple, plasma-like substance encased in a transparent, pulsating shell. When activated, it sends the user into a ghost-like state for a short period - completely imperceptible, but unable to interact with the world.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Perk Artefact
en.st_addon_scope_pk1_obzor_descr:
Compact 1x collimator sight developed in the late 1980s for AK-pattern weapons with a side-mount interface; rugged, utilitarian styling suits close-quarters and short-range work. It operates without conventional batteries (ambient-light/solar collection with tritium illumination for low light) and offers simple day/night reticle modes and a contrast filter for bright conditions. Produced by the Novosibirsk Instrument-Building Plant (NPZ) to a military specification (development at TsKB "Tochpribor"), it's commonly seen in modern Russian service and civilian variants.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: NPZ/TsKB Tochpribor
  •  Magnification: 1x
en.st_addon_scope_1p76_descr:
A Russian prismatic combat sight with a wide field of view and an illuminated etched reticle. It offers parallax-free performance and does not require batteries for daytime use.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: NPZ Optics
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_1p87_descr:
Modern Russian open-reflex sight with a holographic-style reticle, designed for rapid target acquisition at close to mid ranges. Rugged housing and wide field of view make it suitable for both military and law-enforcement small arms applications, and it mounts on standard Picatinny interfaces.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: Valday
  •  Magnification: 1x
en.st_addon_scope_5c_tik_descr:
A comprehensive upgrade package that includes grips, rails, stocks, and other enhancements to improve weapon ergonomics, control, and overall combat effectiveness.
 
 KIT INCLUDES:
  •  Shortened Barrel
  •  Low Profile Foregrip
  •  LA5 LAM
  •  Leupold Carbine Optic Sight
  •  D-EVO 6.0x Scope
  •  Modified Gas Module
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  AK 5C
en.st_addon_scope_monstrum_2x32_descr:
Compact prism scope that delivers true fixed-power magnification in a short, rugged package. Etched prism reticle with illumination for low-light holds makes fast target acquisition and quick follow-ups straightforward; the sight favors simple, practical reticles over complicated ballistic calculators. Low-profile housing mounts on Picatinny rails and is suited for carbines and short-to-mid-range rifles where a snag-free, durable optic is preferred.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Monstrum Tactical
  •  Magnification: 2x
  •  Reticle illumination: press Mark Switch key
en.st_addon_scope_acog_descr:
Advanced Combat Optical Gunsight — a rugged, compact prismatic sight built around a fixed-power tube and a battery-free illumination system (fiber-optic for daylight, tritium for low light). Beloved by many front-line units for its clarity and simplicity, certain ACOG reticles include bullet-drop compensation calibrated for specific rounds so shooters can make fast, first-round hits without dialing turrets. It's the sort of optic that feels like it was designed to be abused — hard, bright, and ready the moment you shoulder the weapon.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Trijicon
  •  Magnification: 2.8x
en.st_prt_o_ballistic_16: Carbon Nanotube Layers w/ Kevlar-29 Fabrics (Reinforced)
en.st_addon_scope_aim_low_descr:
A compact and lightweight red dot sight by Aimpoint, known for its long battery life and high durability. It is widely used for both military and civilian applications due to its precision and reliability.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Aimpoint
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_apsabigo_descr:
A comprehensive upgrade package that includes grips, rails, stocks, and other enhancements to improve weapon ergonomics, control, and overall combat effectiveness.
 
 KIT INCLUDES:
  •  Threaded Barrel (for Suppressor)
  •  Tritium Sights
  •  Ergonomic Pistol Grip
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  APS
en.st_addon_scope_bossxe_descr:
The Wilcox BOSS Xe is a high-power laser aiming device designed for military and law enforcement applications. It features a fully integrated optical bench that houses a smart red-dot sight, visible and near-infrared lasers, and a backup iron sight, all co-aligned. This system allows users to quickly switch between two preset ballistic trajectories without re-zeroing the weapon.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Wilcox Industries
  •  Magnification: 1x
  •  Reticle switch: press Mark Switch key
  •  Laser Toggle
en.st_wpn_ksg_descr:
The KSG-23 is a pump-action shotgun that holds 13 rounds of 23x75 ammunition. It is lightweight and very useful in close-range combat.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All Red Dot scopes for picatinny/weaver. 
  •  Silencer: Swine-23
en.st_addon_scope_c79_descr:
Compact 3.4x optical sight developed by Ernst Leitz Canada (ELCAN) and adopted as the standard optics package for the C7/C8 family of rifles — built to be a rugged, combat-ready mid-range scope with a simple illuminated reticle and practical hold marks tuned for 5.56. Its sealed aluminum housing and straightforward adjustment layout made it popular with military units from the 1980s onward, and it became a workhorse prism/optic on NATO-style carbines long before low-power variable optics became common. The C79's lineage later fed into Elcan's Specter line of dual-mode optics, so its design DNA shows up in several modern combat sights.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Canada
  •  Manufacturer: ELCAN
  •  Magnification: 3.4x
en.st_cortex_scope_desc:
The Vortex Razor HD Gen III 1-10x24 is a premium first focal plane LPVO designed for versatility and precision. It offers a true 1x magnification for close-quarters engagements and extends to 10x for mid-range targets. The scope features an HD optical system with fully multi-coated lenses, providing exceptional clarity and light transmission. Its rugged construction ensures durability in demanding conditions, making it suitable for military, law enforcement, and competitive shooting applications.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Vortex
  •  Magnification: 1–10x
en.st_addon_scope_d0cter_descr:
A premium German-made micro red dot sight known for its crystal-clear optics and compact design. It is widely used on pistols, rifles, and shotguns for fast target engagement.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Germany
  •  Manufacturer: NOBLEX E-Optics
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_devo_descr:
The Leupold D-EVO Offset Prism Optic combined with the LCO red-dot offers a unique dual-optic system designed for rapid target acquisition and mid-range precision. The D-EVO is a 6x magnified prism sight that mounts offset to the side of a primary red-dot sight, such as the LCO, allowing shooters to transition seamlessly between close-quarters engagements and longer-range targets. Together, these optics offer a compact and efficient solution for dynamic shooting scenarios.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Leupold
  •  Magnification: 1x (LCO)/ 6x (D-EVO)
  •  Reticle illumination: press Mark Switch key
en.st_addon_scope_e0t2_descr:
A compact holographic sight designed for rapid target acquisition, featuring night vision compatibility and a single 1 MOA dot for precision aiming. It is lightweight and highly durable, making it a favorite among military and law enforcement.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: EOTech
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_echo1_descr:
Compact thermal reflex sight combining a 1x reflex-like view with digital zoom up to 2x in a short, weapon-mounted package. Uses a low-resolution uncooled VOx thermal array with a 30 Hz refresh rate, giving clear hot-target imagery at short-to-mid ranges even in total darkness; onboard controls and a Picatinny mount make it quick to fit to carbines and PDWs. Lightweight and ruggedized for field use, the sight is optimized for target detection and acquisition rather than fine optical resolution.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Sig Sauer
  •  Magnification: 1x-2x
en.st_addon_scope_ekp8_02_descr:
A refined version of the Kobra reflex sight, offering a reliable, illuminated reticle with adjustable brightness settings. It maintains its robust construction and is favored for Russian military and special forces use.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: Kobra-Axion
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_bandage_descr:
A normal elastic bandage used to stop bleeding and prevent infection of wounds. For regular bleeding, carefully apply the bandage to the wound. For arterial bleeding, urgently tie the bandage around the affected area.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  • first aid T••• A••• L•••
en.st_addon_scope_gee36_descr:
Derived from the G36's carry-handle sighting module, this compact telescopic optic functions as a standalone mid-range scope when mounted on a Picatinny rail. Simple reticle, rugged housing, and combat-grade construction make it suited for quick transitions between close and medium ranges while retaining the original sight's durability. Originally produced for the G36 by Hensoldt (Zeiss lineage), the unit is commonly found repurposed or adapted for other platforms.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Germany

  •  Manufacturer: Hensoldt (produced for Heckler & Koch)
  •  Magnification: 3x
en.st_up_autd_b5_descr: A gas motor is used in self-cocking shotguns for semi-automatic fire.
en.st_wpn_rp_descr:
A modernized and improved version of the PK, the PKM's upgrades were primarily aimed at reducing weight, facilitating easier operation and simplifying production. A very reliable machine gun that remains in use as a front-line infantry and vehicle-mounted weapon with Russia's armed forces. This model has been manually altered to include an enlarged ammo box.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  origin: Soviet Union
  •  manufacturer: Degtyaryov Plant
en.st_up_bar_c3_descr: A peculiar barrel replacement for weapons with good flatness characteristics that improves their accuracy.
en.st_addon_scope_he510_descr:
The Holosun HE510C is an open red-dot sight. It features a 2 MOA dot and 65 MOA circle reticle, offering quick target acquisition and precision aiming. Built with an aluminum housing and titanium hood, it ensures durability and shock resistance. The sight includes a quick-detach mount for easy installation and removal.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States/China
  •  Manufacturer: Holosun
  •  Magnification: 1x
  •  Reticle switch: press Mark Switch key
en.st_addon_scope_kab_up_descr:
A comprehensive upgrade package that includes grips, rails, stocks, and other enhancements to improve weapon ergonomics, control, and overall combat effectiveness.
 
 KIT INCLUDES:
  •  Threaded Barrel (for Suppressor)
  •  Trijicon RMR Sight
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  KS-23M Drozd
en.st_addon_scope_kemper_descr:
PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Firefield
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_kit_fal_leup_descr:
A comprehensive upgrade package that includes grips, rails, stocks, and other enhancements to improve weapon ergonomics, control, and overall combat effectiveness.
 
 KIT INCLUDES:
  •  Fab AG FAL pistol grip
  •  SA-58 Extreme Duty Dust Cover
  •  Picatinny Rail
  •  Vltor CASV Foregrip
  •  21" Barrel
  •  DSA BRS Stock
  •  Aimpoint T1 Sight
  •  Leupold Mark 8 CQBSS Sight
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  SA-58 AUS
en.st_addon_scope_kobra_descr:
A Russian reflex sight known for its durability and four selectable reticles. It provides a fast sight picture with a robust design suited for harsh battlefield conditions.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: Axion
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_kp_sr2_descr:
Compact open-reflex sight developed in the 1990s for the SR-2 / SR-2M "Veresk" family, optimized for the short receiver and folding-stock layout. Its low-profile, rugged housing preserves a small footprint while providing quick target acquisition via a simple red-dot reticle.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: TsNIITochMash
  •  Magnification: 1x
  •  Reticle switch: press Mark Switch key
en.st_addon_scope_lco1_descr:
Leupold Carbine Optic — a rugged red-dot sight designed for patrol rifles and carbines, featuring a crisp 1-MOA dot for precise close-quarters aiming and a wide viewing window for fast target acquisition. Built to be waterproof, fogproof, and shockproof, the LCO stands up to harsh field conditions while maintaining reliable performance.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Leupold
  •  Magnification: 1x
en.st_addon_scope_magpul_pro_descr:
A comprehensive upgrade package that includes grips, rails, stocks, and other enhancements to improve weapon ergonomics, control, and overall combat effectiveness.
 
 KIT INCLUDES:
  •  Magpul PRO 700 Polymer Stock
  •  12 Round Magazines Set
  •  Trijicon RMR Sight
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  Remington M700
en.st_addon_scope_marchf_descr:
A precision Japanese-made riflescope built for long-range work and fast transitions. The illuminated first-focal-plane reticle keeps the subtensions accurate across the range, while the large 52 mm objective and ED glass deliver high contrast and excellent light transmission.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Japan
  •  Manufacturer: DEON Optical Design
  •  Magnification: 3–24x
  •  Reticle illumination: press Mark Switch key
en.st_addon_scope_mepro_descr:
A self-illuminated reflex sight that requires no batteries, using fiber optics and tritium for daytime and low-light visibility. It is rugged, designed for combat use, and provides rapid target acquisition.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Meprolight
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_mod9_descr:
A comprehensive upgrade package that includes grips, rails, stocks, and other enhancements to improve weapon ergonomics, control, and overall combat effectiveness.
 
 KIT INCLUDES:
  •  Threaded Barrel (for Suppressor)
  •  Tritium Sights
  •  Viridian X5L Laser Sight
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  Walther P99
en.st_addon_scope_okp-7_descr:
A modern Russian reflex sight with a unique open design, offering an unobstructed field of view and rapid target acquisition. It is lightweight and highly durable, making it suitable for various combat conditions.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: Russia
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_pgo7gs_descr:
A simple, purpose-built optic paired with the RPG-7 since the 1960s, the PGO-7 gives grenade crews a modest magnification and a ballistic reticle calibrated for common warheads. Compact and straightforward to use, it improves target ID and first-shot hits at extended ranges and was produced in several factories (with licensed copies abroad).
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Soviet Union
  •  Manufacturer: Soviet Union
  •  Magnification: 4x
en.st_addon_scope_pka_descr:
A robust Russian red dot sight designed for use on AK platforms, offering rapid target acquisition with a clear and adjustable reticle. Its durable build makes it ideal for harsh battlefield conditions.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: BelOMO
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_pso2_descr:
A modernized, compact offshoot of the classic PSO family adapted for special sniper platforms (notably the VSS/AS VAL) and short-to-medium ranges. The reticle and stadiametric markings are tuned for closer engagements (the rangefinder markings stop well short of the original 1000-m scale), and the body benefits from updated coatings and manufacturing tweaks for clearer glass and better low-light performance. It serves where the original PSO's long-range BDC is unnecessary — a practical, battlefield-ready optic for quieter, specialized rifles.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Soviet Union/Belarus
  •  Manufacturer: Zenit-BelOMO
  •  Magnification: 4x
  •  Reticle illumination: press Mark Switch key
en.st_addon_scope_rmr_descr:
The Trijicon RMR (Ruggedized Miniature Reflex) is a compact, durable red dot sight designed for pistols and rifles. Its small size and robust construction make it ideal for both military and civilian use.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Trijicon
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_romeo8_descr:
Full-size enclosed red-dot sight designed for carbines and rifles, offering a noticeably larger viewing window than compact tube optics. Its main distinction from the ROMEO4 line is the oversized housing and generous sight picture, combined with selectable reticle options (dot, circle, or combinations) and NVG-compatible brightness settings. Built to handle heavy recoil and hard use, it fills the role of a robust combat-grade optic for rapid engagement at short to medium ranges.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Sig Sauer
  •  Magnification: 1x
  •  Reticle switch: press Mark Switch key
en.st_wpn_pm_bas_descr:
This Soviet legacy is one of the best compact self-defence handguns available. Small, highly reliable, easy to use and easy to maintain, the Pistol Makarova was selected because of its simplicity, easy manufacturing, accuracy and reasonable stopping power. Unfortunately though, it loses out to modern pistols in its class in terms of weight, magazine size and general performance.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  origin: Soviet Union
  •  manufacturer: Izhevsk Mechanical Plant
en.st_addon_scope_silen98_descr:
A modular attachment system that allows various suppressors to be mounted onto compatible firearms, enhancing versatility and sound suppression capabilities.
 
 KIT INCLUDES:
  •  Suppressor Adapter
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  Mauser 98k Forester
en.st_addon_scope_susat_descr:
Sight Unit Small Arms, Trilux — a British service optic developed in the late 1970s to give every rifleman a usable telescopic sight on the new SA80 family. Built with a simple, hard-wearing body and a tritium-illuminated aiming point for low-light use, the SUSAT became the standard sight for the L85/L86 and related weapons before later upgrades began to replace it.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United Kingdom
  •  Manufacturer: Avimo
  •  Magnification: 4x
en.st_addon_scope_swamp_descr:
A comprehensive upgrade package that includes grips, rails, stocks, and other enhancements to improve weapon ergonomics, control, and overall combat effectiveness.
 
 KIT INCLUDES:
  •  Extended Forend
  •  Ergonomic Stock & Pistol Grip
  •  Improved Trigger
  •  Elcan SpecterDR DFOV14-C2 Sight
  •  Trijicon RMR Sight
  •  AN/PEQ-15 LAM Laser Module
  •  45 Round Magazines Set
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  AK-105
en.st_addon_scope_t12_descr:
Compact weapon-mounted thermal imager offering a 30 Hz refresh rate from an 80x60 FLIR Lepton sensor. Provides a false-color image polarity, on-screen reticle overlay and quick-detach Picatinny mounting for weapon or handheld use, but the small sensor limits fine identification to short–mid ranges. Built in a metal, O-ring sealed housing with on-board image processing and battery operation for rugged low-visibility scanning.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: United States
  •  Manufacturer: Torrey Pines Logic
  •  Magnification: 1x
en.st_addon_scope_upg220_descr:
A comprehensive upgrade package that includes grips, rails, stocks, and other enhancements to improve weapon ergonomics, control, and overall combat effectiveness.
 
 KIT INCLUDES:
  •  Threaded Barrel (for Suppressor)
  •  Tritium Sights
  •  Viridian X5L laser Sight
  •  12 Round Magazine
 
 COMPATIBILITY:
  •  SIG P220
en.st_cigarettes_lucky_descr:
: A pack of American-made Lucky Strike cigarettes, known for their rich flavor and historical significance. Highly coveted in the Zone, they offer a nostalgic taste of the outside world.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  Stimulant
  •  Alcohol Sobering Effect
en.isg_scientific_outfit_description: Originally commissioned by the first iteration of UNISG from one of Kiev's defense research institutes for research in the Zone. The special treatment materials provide enhanced protection and durability against radiation and anomalous effects.\n \n
en.light_merc_outfit_description: A well-kept Interceptor Anomalous Body Armor System sold directly to the Mercenaries from various Western sources. While a little uncomfortable, this suit is overall better than the standard model circulating around the Zone.\n \n
en.st_ecologist_outfit_blue_description: High quality modified SSP-99 suit. A Kevlar underlayer has been added to the suit to reduce the risk of suit puncture from hostile mutants. The "Sapphire" model provides excellent protection against short-range psi-emissions.\n \n
en.hybrid_outfit_description: A suit created by one of Kiev's defense research institutes for the purpose of guarding researchers in the Zone. However, it is clear the suit was created on a budget. As such, none of these suits were ever officially commissioned by the Ukrainian government, although a few managed to find their way into the Zone.\n \n
en.isg_exo_outfit_description: The official version of the UNISG exoskeleton released in limited quantities by special order of the United Nations. While it still possesses many of the design flaws of other exoskeletons, it's nonetheless seen as a great alternative to light command and scout vehicles.\n \n
en.cs_scientific_outfit_good_description: An unofficial version of the SEVA scientific suit that has been extensively improved by Novikov, Clear Sky's resident technician. Extremely comfortable to move around in, making it a high-demand suit among some factions.\n \n
en.monolith_exo_outfit_description: High-tech armor consisting of a steel-reinforced Kevlar jumpsuit and a tactical vest with Class IV inserts, all carried by powered limbs and servomotors. Study samples have revealed that its servos are made of alloys and materials unknown to any field of science in any other part of the world. Wearing this suit feels like being buried under the concrete of the Sarcophagus itself; terrifyingly claustrophobic for most stalkers but eerily comforting for true servants of the Monolith.\n \n
en.monolith_exolight_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. A favorite among the Monolith's battle preachers and tactical masters who claim the confines of the suit to be as comforting as the Sarcophagus itself.\n \n
en.army_outfit_description: A poor protective outfit contracted by government bureaucrats outsourcing to the lowest bidder. Typically issued to helicopter pilots, tank drivers, rear-echelon personnel, conscripts and enlisted servicemen.\n \n
en.light_voen_outfit_description: An Interceptor Anomalous Body Armor System that ended up in the hands of the Military. Compared to the standard model, this suit is slightly more ergonomic and boasts greater protection against psychic interference.\n \n
en.merc_jackal_outfit_description: A light tactical suit, nicknamed "Jackal" after the now dead Merc leader who was assigned to crack open the X-8 laboratory over six years ago. Being marvelously comfortable with a fairly sophisticated weight distribution system, it is a favorite among more experienced Mercenaries.\n \n
en.dolg_red_outfit_description: A somewhat heavier variant of the PS5-M universal protection suit, specifically designed for Duty and intending to hunt mutants. A small amount of ballistic protection is sacrificed in favor of much better protection against common mutant attacks. Compares well even to modern Duty suits.\n \n
en.exo_merc_wood_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. This variant includes an old CBRN that has unfortunately not been modified for the Zone.\n \n
en.monolith_nbc_outfit_description: A Monolith suit with the vest switched out for a special pocketed load-bearing alternative. A solid suit for a scavenger on a budget.\n \n
en.light_dolg_outfit_description: An old IBA suit imported from the West and modified for the Zone's conditions. Uncomfortably heavy, the Duty variant seems to have received extensive upgrades for protection against chemical weapons.\n \n
en.scientific_outfit_description: Designed and produced by one of Kiev's defense research institutes and represents an excellent alternative to hand-made stalker suits. It combines a CBRN jumpsuit with a specially-treated class II Kevlar vest. Due to the quality of materials used, this bodysuit sports good durability within ambient anomalous fields.\n \n
en.isg_exolight_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. A rare sight, since most ISG operatives prefer increased mobility over raw protective power.\n \n
en.cs_exolight_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. These outfits are smuggled in from Ukrainian factories near the borders of the Zone.\n \n
en.bandit_novice_alt_outfit_description: Jackets like this first entered circulation among Bandits thanks to Quartermaster Morgan, a corrupt supplier who formerly belonged to the Duty faction. Offers protection comparable to a standard Military service outfit. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.light_isg_outfit_description: A strong but somewhat outdated combat suit. Considered the bare minimum of what should be worn in situations where conflict with armed SSU forces seems inevitable.\n \n
en.monolith_proto_exo_outfit_description: An armor made by either Monolithians or an unknown manufacturer. It has been specially modified to fit with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection but is also considerably lighter.\n \n
en.travel_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. This particular outfit is of high quality and is intended for extended exploration within the Zone. A suit of choice for many artefact and mutant hunters.\n \n
en.svoboda_heavy_outfit_description: A set of Spetsnaz body armor that has been "liberated" for use in the Freedom faction. This suit has been treated with "Sovereign", an anomalous compound invented by the faction. Any Freedom member expected to help defend the Barrier will do their best to get their hands on this suit.\n \n
en.svoboda_novice_outfit_description: A camouflaged leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and combat boots. Such attire is typically only worn by Freedom members. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.merc_sunset_outfit_description: A Mercenary Light Combat Suit modified for use in the Zone. This suit is a genuine improvement for most newer Mercenaries and not a bad alternative to the faction's special tactical suits.\n \n
en.csky_medium_outfit_description: A heavier but still comfortable outfit worn by members of Clear Sky that provides more than adequate protection, especially in combat.\n \n
en.exo_outfit_description: An experimental sample of a military exoskeleton consisting of a steel-reinforced Kevlar jumpsuit and a tactical vest with Class IV inserts, all carried by powered limbs and servomotors. Was never mass-produced due to extraordinary cost and some design flaws though small batches are made in underground facilities outside Ukraine.\n \n
en.stalker_graphite_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. It was independently designed by a few optimistic stalkers who were expecting to take measurements of psy-storms out in the open. Naturally, this endeavor failed, but their designs were later recovered and used to produce a stalker suit able to shelter the wearer's nervous system.\n \n
en.isg_lcs_outfit_description: A "Light Combat Suit" model adapted for use by UNISG troops. Its design is based on the one used by the special forces of Western armies.\n \n
en.dolg_heavy_proto_exo_outfit_description: A PSZ-9d armored bodysuit, specially designed to be fitted with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it sacrifices raw ballistic protection for greater functionality in anomalous fields.\n \n
en.st_helm_rp_bala_descr: A regular black fabric balaclava. Stalkers will often drape it around their mouth and nose to avoid breathing in toxic or radioactive particles, but this protection is unreliable at best.\n \n
en.svoboda_light_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by Freedom craftsmen. This comfortable bodysuit has been treated with a special solution known as 'Horizon', to increase resistance to chemical anomalies.\n \n
en.svoboda_light_proto_exo_outfit_descripti: A Wind of Freedom bodysuit, specially designed to be fitted with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection but is also considerably lighter.\n \n
en.specops_merc_outfit_description: Spetsnaz body armor acquired and repurposed by the Zone's Mercenaries. Provides good physical protection while also being remarkably comfortable to wear. Often worn by Mercenaries who used to work for the SSU.\n \n
en.dolg_heavy_outfit_description: Developed by defence research institutes in Kiev, the first iteration of this Duty bodysuit began as a simple old set of Spetsnaz PSZ-9 body armor, modified for the Zone. These days, this armor's latest design is capable of stopping a rifle bullet and boasts great protection against the Zone's myriad horrors. It is uncommon for armor as prized as this to be issued to any Duty member who hasn't proven themselves in some significant manner.\n \n
en.greh_ps5_outfit_description: This outfit's design is based on the PS5-M universal protection suit, though it is unknown how it has come into the possession of Sin. Nevertheless, Duty is not happy to know their armor designs are being appropriated by Zone worshiping fanatics and the recovery of these suits are an unspoken priority. The suit itself is of good quality and is more than adequate for the Zone's conditions.\n \n
en.nbc_merc_outfit_description: A Mercenary 'Twilight' suit with the vest switched out for a special pocketed load-bearing alternative. A solid suit for a scavenger on a budget.\n \n
en.light_loner_outfit_description: An old IBA suit imported from the West and modified for the Zone's conditions.\n \n
en.st_helm_respirator_gp5_descr: Originally built with the intention to protect the Soviet people from radioactive fallout. The filters of this GP-5 have been restored with a great deal of care. Compared to gas mask kits of similar price and availability, this reconditioned respiratory device boasts greater protection against radiation, but an even greater failure rate in toxic environments.\n \n
en.renegade_exo_outfit_description: One of a few exoskeletons assembled by the Renegades back in 2011 to help secure their final push into Clear Sky's base. Unfortunately, they were virtually wiped out before their final victory ever came, thanks to the interference of a Mercenary with a trench coat and a marksman rifle. Modern Renegades who wear this armor always go into battle like a man possessed, eager to mete vengeance upon their most hated foes.\n \n
en.renegade_scientific_outfit_description: This comfortable bodysuit, originally intended for research in the Zone, makes for a great alternative to the various other light combat or hand-made suits readily available within the Zone. Compared to a standard SEVA suit, it provides improved protection against thermal hazards and blunt trauma. However, its other protective qualities are inferior to the original SEVA.\n \n
en.st_helm_protective_descr: A high quality curtain helmet made by the same Defense Research Institute as the SSP-99 and SEVA bodysuits. Allows for complete isolation from the conditions of an anomalous environment. A closed-cycle air filtration system supplies the wearer with consistently cool, clean air.\n \n
en.st_helm_battle_descr: An aluminum/titanium helmet with a cloth exterior for camouflage. It comes with pockets that can be filled with additional armor pieces. This helmet is commonly used among military stalkers and Spetsnaz, who typically use it in tandem with PSZ-series body armor. Overall, the M12 Sphere Helmet provides some of the best combat-oriented protection in the Zone.\n \n
en.merc_wolven_outfit_description: Sometimes nicknamed the "Wolven", this outfit is a comfortable special tactical suit, used by Mercenaries in both exploratory expeditions and stealth operations.\n \n
en.stalker_bear_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. The suit's improved protection against ballistics has been achieved through an unnamed home-made anomalous compound.\n \n
en.stalker_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. The Sunrise is one of the oldest stalker suits in existence. It is also widely considered the bare minimum protection a stalker should have for serious trips into the Zone.\n \n
en.merc_scientific_outfit_description: A variant of the SEVA bodysuit that was commissioned by Mercenaries. Used by veteran Mercs on artefact-hunting contracts. Good protection for a SEVA suit but missing some of the newer psy-protection upgrades available in the newer SEVA models.\n \n
en.helm_ach7ex_descr: The ACH-7 (Advanced Compound Headgear, 7 Parts) is a more modern version of the surplus steel helmet. The ACH-7EX comes with the inclusion of a respiratory system, boasting chemical and radiological protection somewhere between that of a GP-5 and a PBF kit.\n \n
en.helm_ppm88_descr: A Russian gas mask well suited for CBRN ecological hazards. In the Zone, it can be considered a functional and spiritual successor to the GP-5.\n \n
en.dolg_exolight_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. This suit is typically found in the hands of elite Duty warriors, especially those assigned to guard VIPs or launch exceedingly dangerous raids on enemy stalkers.\n \n
en.exo_wood_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. This model includes an early-generation CBRN jumpsuit lined with Kevlar.\n \n
en.st_ecologist_outfit_yello_description: The SSP-99 is a current-generation CBRN suit specially designed for the Zone's conditions and is used by scientific expeditions for extended forays into anomalous fields. When combined with the SSP-99 screen helmet, it includes an integrated air-filtering and air-conditioning system. The "Topaz" variant provides increased protection from radiation.\n \n
en.bandit_novice_outfit_description: A black leather jacket commonly associated with the Zone's bandits and crooks. Mostly worn to intimidate rookies from other factions, it provides very little protection. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.military_proto_exo_outfit_description: The very first iteration of the military exoskeleton, modified to fit with the servos and batteries of a modern exoskeleton. Compared to a third-generation exo, it sacrifices raw ballistic protection for greater functionality in anomalous fields.\n \n
en.ecologist_guard_outfit_description: A basic suit originally intended as spare equipment for Professor Hermann's "Iskra Scientific Research and Investigation Team", it has since become a relatively cheap protective bodysuit for Ecologist guards working all over the Zone.\n \n
en.exo_dolg_red_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. This particular variant is typically found in the hands of elite Duty huntsmen, where its greater defense against mutants is utilized.\n \n
en.greh_exo_outfit_description: High-tech armor consisting of a steel-reinforced Kevlar jumpsuit and a tactical vest with Class IV inserts, all carried by powered limbs and servomotors. Study samples have revealed that its servos are made of alloys and materials unknown to any field of science in any other part of the world. Wearing this suit creates an unnatural feeling of bliss and calm within the wearer, although whether this sense of security is genuine or not remains to be understood.\n \n
en.isg_outfit_description: Based on a fairly standard suit design used by Western special forces, outfits like this are common to many militaries throughout the world. This is the bare minimum a UNISG operative can expect to be fitted with.\n \n
en.bandit_scientific_outfit_description: Bandits have never been terribly interested in the science and research of anomalous fields, but nevertheless, need protective suits such as these to organize ambushes and attacks on stalkers within anomalous fields and radiation pockets.\n \n
en.dolg_scientific_red_outfit_description: A lighter variant of the Duty scientific suit that has shed almost half its weight at the cost of slightly decreased protection. The ballistic armor has been replaced by a class IIA vest with numerous pockets.\n \n
en.dolg_voin_outfit_description: A modern, updated variant of the original PS5-M universal protection bodysuit. It is more than adequate for all of the Zone's conditions as well as being markedly comfortable for a Duty suit. Originally intended to replace its predecessor entirely, its higher production cost put this goal on hold in the face of Duty's multitude of recent losses.\n \n
en.monolith_scientific_outfit_description: The Monolith scientific suit includes a flak vest of unknown origin and features unique modifications for greater resistances to radiation at the cost of being more vulnerable to psy-fields. Curiously, according to some legends, these suits are most often worn by the soldiers who defend the Brain Scorcher in Radar, although no one outside the Monolith faction can easily confirm.\n \n
en.ecolog_proto_exo_outfit_description: A unique armor system composed of an exoskeleton-powered SSP-99M jumpsuit with an anomaly-resistant Class IIA Kevlar vest placed on top. The durable exo-frames are capable of providing mechanical support for carrying heavy loads even through anomaly fields. Ideal for aiding researchers during deep incursions into the Zone.\n \n
en.merc_coyote_outfit_description: A medium tactical suit, nicknamed the "Coyote". Widely considered the successor of the LTS-J body suit. Naturally, its price is higher than that of its predecessor, but this won't stop most Mercs from springing for it when they are able.\n \n
en.svoboda_exo_outfit_description: An excellent bodysuit produced by Freedom’s finest artisans consisting of a steel-reinforced Kevlar jumpsuit and a tactical vest with Class IV inserts, all carried by powered limbs and servomotors. While not always the most practical suit to maintain, it is undeniably powerful, and as such is entrusted only to Freedom's finest warriors and guardsmen.\n \n
en.freedom_nosorog_outfit_desc: A next generation exoskeleton, consisting of a doubly reinforced Exosuit. Rumored to have been the brainchild of a now-deceased stalker named "Fang", whose designs were later claimed by all sorts of powerful factions across the Zone. If regular exoskeletons allow stalkers to feel like a light vehicle, the Nosorog almost makes them believe they're a main battle tank, able to withstand incredible punishment while carrying tremendous ordnance.\n \n
en.merc_scout_outfit_description: Nicknamed the "Scout", this outfit is an LC-F suit modified to fill a more specialized niche. A very comfortable suit. Its modifications make it a good choice for Mercenaries who want to explore the Zone's anomalies on a budget.\n \n
en.helm_ranger_descr: Using the GP-10 as its model, this gas mask was designed primarily to protect the wearer from Controllers and Poltergeists.\n \n
en.dolg_scientific_wood_outfit_description: As a token of goodwill and to help cover their research costs, Clear Sky occasionally makes coveted "Pathfinder" suits on commission for use among the Duty faction. Altered according to Duty's needs, its weight has been reduced by almost half at the expense of protection. Despite this, it's an excellent and comfortable outfit for Duty's more curious members.\n \n
en.st_ecologist_outfit_grn_description: High quality modified SSP-99 suit. A Kevlar underlayer has been added to reduce the risk of suit puncture from hostile mutants. Provides improved protection from biological anomalies compared to the SSP-99.\n \n
en.dolg_novice_outfit_description: A dark leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and combat boots. Formerly used only as relaxed wear, these simple suits were never intended for use by those on active service. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.dolg_heavy_redline_outfit_description: This suit was a favorite of the late Lieutenant Colonel Shulga, who rarely ventured outside his operational base in Yanov, yet constantly feared assassination at the hands of Freedom. Thus, heavy protection like this fitted his needs perfectly. As fate would have it, he'd instead later be presumed dead after leading a mass assault into Pripyat with his contingent of troops. Veteran Duty member will occasionally wear suits like this to battle in his honor.\n \n
en.merc_sun_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, made by local craftsmen. Its design bears resemblance to a Monolith X-18 suit, an outfit the Mercs no doubt tried to salvage from their costly skirmishes in Limansk.\n \n
en.dolg_outfit_description: An old version of the Duty bodysuit, better suited for recruits who may prefer the familiarity of stalker suits over frontline battle armor.\n \n
en.stalker_salamander_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. An economical choice with great upgrade potential. Ironically, for being dubbed the "Salamander", it demonstrates a poor ability to safely channel heat.\n \n
en.cs_stalker_outfit_description: Unlike other suits in the CS-1, 2 and 3 series, this body armor was only created after the faction's massive losses at the Chernobyl NPP. It is widely considered to be the first proper successor to the CS-1 suit as it focuses on keeping the wearer safe from anomalous environments over armoring them for battle.\n \n
en.isg_proto_exo_outfit_description: A UN-branded special tactical bodysuit, specially designed to be fitted with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection but is also considerably lighter.\n \n
en.bandit_scientific_dark_outfit_descriptio: A SEVA-like suit made from scratch by one of the Bandits' gifted craftsmen. Compared to the looted SEVAs that are brought in and restored, this patchwork outfit boasts average protection against anomalies while retaining a surprisingly low weight. Additionally, it's rather stylish and comfy, allowing it to become a hit among the criminals who can afford it.\n \n
en.ace_outfit_description: One of the most technologically advanced bodysuits in the Zone. The ACE armor system incorporates a next-generation CBRN and a pocketed Kevlar vest with Class III plate inserts. Using state of the art materials such as selectively-permeable coatings, this suit manages both maximal anomaly protection and wearer comfort. Used by only the wealthiest Mercenaries.\n \n
en.merc_exo_outfit_description: An experimental sample of a military exoskeleton of western production consisting of a steel-reinforced Kevlar jumpsuit and a tactical vest with Class IV inserts, all carried by powered limbs and servomotors.. While not always the most practical suit to maintain in the Zone, it simultaneously allows the wearer to be both heavily armed and extremely well-armored, with enough strength to carry a week’s worth of supplies. Most often worn by veteran Mercenary captains expected to lead their men through hellish conditions.\n \n
en.st_helm_bandana_descr: An ordinary fabric bandana worn by conscripts, support corps and rear-echelon troops in the Military. Stalkers will often drape it around their mouth and nose to avoid breathing in toxic or radioactive particles, but this protection is unreliable at best.\n \n
en.merc_nighthunter_outfit_description: Sometimes nicknamed the "Night Hunter", this outfit is a slightly heavy special tactical suit, used by Mercenaries in both nocturnal and urban combat scenarios.\n \n
en.bandit_nbc_outfit_description: A 'Sunset' bandit suit with the vest switched out for a special pocketed load-bearing alternative. A solid suit for a scavenger on a budget.\n \n
en.exo_dolg_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. This suit is typically found in the hands of elite Duty warriors, especially those assigned to guard VIPs or launch exceedingly dangerous raids.\n \n
en.merc_fighter_outfit_description: Referred to as the "Fighter" by Mercenaries, this suit is a variation of the standard LC outfit. It can perform surprisingly well in operations where stealth and speed are imperative.\n \n
en.csky_novice_outfit_description: A soft blue leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. These simple suits are assembled in large quantities by Clear Sky's tailors for use within the faction. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.stalker_predator_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. An overall upgrade over the "Drought" suit and significantly more comfortable to wear. The "Predator" enjoys popularity among greenhorns and experts alike thanks to its low maintenance costs and great modification potential.\n \n
en.svoboda_exolight_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. Only the most respected members of Freedom will ever be found wearing a suit like this.\n \n
en.st_ecologist_outfit_orange_description: The SSP-99 is a current-generation CBRN suit specially designed for the Zone's conditions and is used by scientific expeditions for extended forays into anomalous fields. When combined with the SSP-99 screen helmet, it includes an integrated air-filtering and air-conditioning system. Its design is specifically tailored for maximum durability in thermal, chemical, and electrical fields.\n \n
en.exo_merc_grass_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. This variant includes an asbestos-weave jumpsuit.\n \n
en.exolight_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time.\n \n
en.exo_merc_urban_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. Additionally, it is only useful in melee and ranged fights, since it offers no anomaly protection whatsoever. An outfit of choice for the captains and bodyguards of Mercenary companies operating in areas like Limansk or Pripyat.\n \n
en.trenchcoat_brown_outfit_description: A long, thick overcoat worn over a butchered scientist suit. Although the closed-cycle system and several other features seem to have been stripped off, the end result still compares favorably to many basic stalker suits.\n \n
en.exo_dolg_urban_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. Additionally, it is only useful in melee and ranged fights, since it offers no anomaly protection whatsoever. Old and exceedingly heavy, it's possible that suits like this were used by the Duty faction to raid Pripyat and clash with Freedom back in 2012.\n \n
en.merc_exolight_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. An outfit of choice for the captains and bodyguards of Mercenary companies requiring top of the line ballistic protection.\n \n
en.merc_scientific_armored_outfit_descripti: A variant of the SEVA bodysuit that was commissioned by Mercenaries. Used by veteran mercenaries who are expected to guide researchers and artefact hunters in the more dangerous parts of the Zone.\n \n
en.wastelander_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. This particular outfit is unusually light compared to average stalker suits but possesses a spacious Class IIIA vest and lightweight inserts. It's essentially a nimbler, stalker-made Berill-5M suit.\n \n
en.csky_heavy_outfit_description: Formerly the standard outfit issued to Clear Sky assault squads, after the faction’s infamous failure against the NPP in 2011, this armor serves as a reminder that even an excellent protective bodysuit cannot guarantee safety on the wrong side of the perimeter.\n \n
en.novice_outfit_tourist_description: A warm leather anorak with an integrated backpack, worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. It seems the previous owner really loved hiking. The educated choice of materials and weight management system makes this more useful than most leather jackets. Still an inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.cs_scientific_outfit_description: An unofficial version of the SEVA scientific suit, fresh off the smuggling route. Typically used by groups of the Clear Sky faction.\n \n
en.st_helm_m40_descr: Provides respectable radiation and chemical protection for extended periods, even after suffering damage. Comes equipped with a multilayered 40-millimetre breathing filter.\n \n
en.light_freedom_outfit_description: Rumored to be supplied to the Freedom faction directly from an unknown source, this suit is based on the somewhat outdated IBA system but modified for greater comfort when operating in the Zone's conditions.\n \n
en.stalker_proto_exo_outfit_description: A stalker bodysuit, specially designed to be fitted with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection but is also considerably lighter.\n \n
en.military_outfit_description: An excellent protective suit for the Zone. This heavy military-grade armor is designed for major battle operations in anomalous areas. Comprises a PSZ-12p heavy military armored suit, built-in compensation suit, and an anomalous protection bodysuit. Although originally intended for the sole use of Military stalkers and experienced Spetsnaz operators, this armor occasionally finds its way into factions outside of the Military through unknown means.\n \n
en.helm_m50_descr: An M50 gas mask tweaked for better functionality in the Zone. Overall, it is considered an improvement over the M40.\n \n
en.st_helm_hardhat_descr: An outdated steel army helmet with a retractable visor.\n \n
en.greh_armored_outfit_description: A comfortable and ergonomic arming suit used to outfit Sin's followers en masse. It consists of a ballistic vest over an environmental bodysuit. These suits do a good job fortifying the already durable warriors of Sin.\n \n
en.freedom_exo_vineleaf_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. This variant includes an old CBRN jumpsuit lined with Kevlar and an additional attachment slot.\n \n
en.exo_dolg_wood_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. This variant includes an older CBRN jumpsuit with Kevlar weave and additional steel reinforcement. Typically found with elite Duty members, particularly those conducting short operations in the northern reaches of the Zone.\n \n
en.redline_novice_outfit_description: A snazzy bright red leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.st_helm_exo_descr: The ExoHelmet is a thick steel helmet produced in the same Ukrainian factories as the exoskeleton armors. The accompanying gas mask is a British SF10, which can easily be found on the surplus markets after they were replaced for the General Service Respirator (GSR).\n \n
en.dolg_scout_outfit_description: An old Duty suit developed alongside the PS5-M universal protection outfit intended to protect Duty scouts in the early days of the Zone's existence. However, the only parts of the Zone left to be explored are far too dangerous for a light bodysuit like this. As such, these are mostly retired, kept in reserve only to outfit Duty recruits in desperate circumstances.\n \n
en.csky_light_novice_outfit_description: A light suit worn by Clear Sky's novice researchers. It provides more than adequate protection from the anomalous phenomena found within the Swamps.\n \n
en.stalker_drought_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. For a stalker suit, the "Drought" possesses above-average ballistic protection, but is considerably less comfortable to wear.\n \n
en.csky_light_outfit_description: An upgrade to the CS-1 bodysuit intended for conducting rapid search operations in areas of low risk.\n \n
en.bandit_exo_outfit_description: A stolen military exoskeleton customized by outlaws to attack well-guarded facilities and to plunder well-armed stalkers. More martially inclined aspiring Bandit Lords tend to favor this armor, using it as a stark and unquestionable symbol of their strength and authority over lesser criminals.\n \n
en.st_ecologist_outfit_white_description: The SSP-99 is a current-generation CBRN suit specially designed for the Zone's conditions and is used by scientific expeditions for extended forays into anomalous fields. When combined with the SSP-99 screen helmet, it includes an integrated air-filtering and air-conditioning system. Its design is specifically tailored for maximum durability in thermal and chemical fields. The "Diamond" variant also gives superior protection from electricity.\n \n
en.cs_stalker_proto_exo_outfit_description: A set of CS-2b body armor that has been specially modified to fit with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection but is also considerably lighter.\n \n
en.helm_ach7_descr: The ACH-7 (Advanced Compound Headgear, 7 Parts) is a more modern version of the surplus steel helmet. Though, its lack of a respiratory device makes it an extremely poor choice for the Zone's conditions.\n \n
en.dolg_specops_red_outfit_description: A set of former Spetsnaz body armor, bolstered with more substantial protection. An excellent combat bodysuit, although it has an infamous failure rate in areas of extreme anomalous activity. Despite this, Duty's veterans claim you do not need protection from anomalies once you know how to spot them.\n \n
en.dolg_scientific_outfit_description: The Duty faction version of the standard SEVA suit. Like the standard suit variant, it consists of a durable anomaly-resistant CBRN jumpsuit and a specially-treated Class II Kevlar vest. A solid suit for extended dives into anomaly fields.\n \n
en.novice_outfit_camo_description: A warm leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. Formerly owned by a typical firearm enthusiast, it sports several advantages over an ordinary jacket, namely a camouflage pattern, higher quality material and more pockets to hold belongings. Still an inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.nosorog_outfit_description: A next generation exoskeleton, consisting of a doubly reinforced Exosuit. Rumored to have been the brainchild of a now-deceased stalker named "Fang", whose designs were later claimed by all sorts of powerful factions across the Zone. If regular exoskeletons allow stalkers to feel like a light vehicle, the Nosorog almost makes them believe they're a main battle tank, able to withstand incredible punishment while carrying tremendous ordnance.\n \n
en.bandit_exolight_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. Both imposing and highly intimidating in appearance, this outfit is a favorite among the Zone's most powerful outlaws.\n \n
en.bandit_ps5_outfit_description: Armor like this first entered circulation among Bandits thanks to Quartermaster Morgan, a corrupt supplier who formerly belonged to the Duty faction. It gives up some of its physical protection for greater comfort and defense against anomalies. These suits are a major sore point for the Duty faction, and getting them out of the hands of anarchists, criminals and murderers is an unspoken priority.\n \n
en.banditmerc_outfit_description: A light combat suit obtained to help armor local Bandit gangs. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. In a twist of dramatic irony, it appears whoever supplied these suits were ripping off their customers as the build quality is significantly worse.\n \n
en.cs_exo_outfit_description: An experimental version of the army exoskeleton smuggled into the Zone from Ukrainian factories before being refurbished by Clear Sky technicians. Generally, suits like these are held in reserve, to be deployed only in times of true crisis. That said, especially trusted comrades and associates of the faction may eventually receive permission to acquire such a suit for their own personal uses, although not without great cost.\n \n
en.dolg_exo_outfit_description: Excellent high-tech armor consisting of a steel-reinforced Kevlar jumpsuit and a tactical vest with Class IV inserts, all carried by powered limbs and servomotors. Domestically developed by one of the research institutes, specifically for the Duty faction. It is slowly being phased out in favor of cheaper, more practical suits, and the few Exosuits in active service tend to be in the hands of Duty's oldest veterans.\n \n
en.dolg_pantsir_outfit_description: An "in-between" suit that is more protective than Duty's regular PS5 universal protection suit, yet cheaper and easier to produce than a proper set of PSZ-9d body armor. Ideal for skirmishes, its relative ease of maintenance and repair is a great boon to Dutyers far away from friendly territory, such as the investigators who venture up into Yanov or beyond.\n \n
en.ecolog_exo_outfit_description: An official version of the military exoskeleton, intended for use by the Ecologists' guards. The suit feels almost impervious to small arms, and its advanced servomotors make it an excellent choice for those in entrenched defensive positions, as it greatly aids in the use of heavy defensive weaponry. When out in the field, wearers of this suit are more commonly used to carrying ludicrous amounts of batteries, LLMCs, AAMs and other supplies an accompanying scientist might need when exploring the Zone.\n \n
en.st_ecologist_outfit_red_description: High quality modified SSP-99 suit. A Kevlar underlayer has been added to the suit to reduce the risk of suit puncture from hostile mutants. The "Ruby" model provides improved protection against melee attacks and includes an integrated wound healing system.\n \n
en.greh_trenchcoat_outfit_description: A long, thick, bloodstained overcoat worn over a butchered scientist suit. Although the closed-cycle system and several other features seem to have been stripped off, the end result still compares favorably to many basic stalker suits.\n \n
en.st_helm_metro_descr: Believed to have first been created by a faction known as "Final Day", this helm resembles a Czech M10 Gas Mask with heavy armor plating modifications. When the faction was dissolved, these helmets gradually entered into circulation.\n \n
en.helm_spartan_descr: A helmet commissioned by extremely influential stalkers, based off the design of the 'Apocalypse' helmet formerly used by the faction, "Final Day". In comparison, this helmet is lighter and more protective than its predecessor, comparing favorably to modern combat helmets such as the 'Sphere' series.\n \n
en.isg_merc_outfit_description: An improvement over the original Mk-1 design, this outfit is much better suited for the Zone's conditions. These improvements were commissioned by UNISG based on the limited data that could be recovered from their less fortunate predecessors.\n \n
en.isg_lcs_camo_outfit_description: A modified "Light Combat Suit" model adapted for use in the Zone by UNISG troops. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. Light weight and comfortable.\n \n
en.isg_lcs_urban_outfit_description: A modified "Light Combat Suit" model adapted for use in battle by UNISG troops. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. Compares favorably in combat compared to many other outfits in its class but still falls short of what a Spetsnaz operator would be wearing.\n \n
en.light_monolit_outfit_description: An Interceptor Anomalous Body Armor System that ended up in the hands of the Monolith. Compared to the standard model, this suit is slightly more ergonomic and boasts greater protection against psychic interference.\n \n
en.merc_combat_scientific_outfit_descriptio: As a token of goodwill and to help cover their research costs, Clear Sky occasionally makes coveted "Pathfinder" suits on commission for use among the Zone's Mercenaries. Compared to the stock SEVA suit, this model is superior across the board. Comes with a built-in Class IIIA ballistic vest.\n \n
en.merc_outfit_description: The "Light Combat" suit is one of the most common outfits used by the Zone's Mercenaries. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. Adequate for basic operations, but quality is lacking when compared to standard Spetsnaz gear.\n \n
en.st_helm_tactic_descr: A modern, high-quality helmet with a built-in air filtration system based on Western models. The design consists of several layers of Kevlar and composite dielectric materials. Most commonly used by the Zone's Spetsnaz operators, especially in conjunction with the PS5 body armor series.\n \n
en.military_exolight_outfit_description: Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. Considered too cumbersome by the Ukrainian government, these suits are never issued to any unit or individual. Thus soldiers, Military stalkers and the occasional veteran must always find and pay for it through their own means.\n \n
en.monolith_outfit_description: A mysterious bodysuit associated with the Monolith faction. Previously of unknown origin, scores of these suits were found in containers and lockers underneath the X18 facility in Dark Valley. There are theories among stalkers that these suits predate the creation of the Zone.\n \n
en.monolith_scientific_light_outfit_descrip: A SEVA-like suit made from scratch by an unknown craftsman. It is unknown what role this suit fills within the Monolith faction as a whole, although it's been speculated that its purpose is largely ceremonial.\n \n
en.nbc_freedom_outfit_description: A Sentinel of Freedom suit with the vest switched out for a special pocketed load-bearing alternative. A solid suit for a scavenger on a budget.\n \n
en.nbc_outfit_description: A 'Sunrise' stalker suit with the vest switched out for a special pocketed load-bearing alternative. A solid suit for a scavenger on a budget.\n \n
en.svoboda_scientific_outfit_description: A suit of "Sentinel" body armor specially produced by Freedom craftsmen for observing anomalous phenomena in the Zone. The specially treated materials provide good protection and suit durability during prolonged exploration of anomalous fields.\n \n
en.renegademerc_outfit_description: A light combat suit obtained through shady means to help armor local groups of Renegades. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. Surprisingly pleasant to wear.\n \n
en.specops_bandit_outfit_description: A set of PS5 series body armor with beryllium sputter, acquired through shady means by the Zone's Bandits. Although the suit shows visible signs of irreparable wear and tear, it's still an excellent choice for battle.\n \n
en.stalker_autumn_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. Favored among newer artefact hunters and creative hit men, the "Autumn" allows easier traversal of chemical anomalous fields.\n \n
en.ghillie_outfit_description: This suit is designed to simulate a ‘heap of foliage.’ Usually, this type of suit is worn by snipers and hunters to blend in with the environment and hide themselves from enemies. Ghillie suits are commercially produced, but usually both military personnel and civilians customize and improve them to suit their needs.\n \n
en.bandit_sun_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, made by local craftsmen. Its design bears resemblance to a Monolith X-18 suit with its conventional NBC features stripped down in favor of greater insulation from thermal and electrical threats. Mostly used by Bandits in Dark Valley to clear out the poltergeists that occasionally escape from the local lab.\n \n
en.nomad_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. This particular outfit is of high quality and feels exceptionally light and comfortable to wear. It is clear that whoever assembled this armor was highly familiar with the Zone's nature and evidently put a great deal of pride and care into their work.\n \n
en.novice_outfit_description: A warm leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. The outfit of choice among naive or inexperienced stalkers. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.nbc_dolg_outfit_description: A PS5-M suit with the vest switched out for a special pocketed load-bearing alternative. A solid suit for a scavenger on a budget.\n \n
en.cs_nbc_outfit_description: A CS-2b stalker suit with the vest switched out for a special pocketed load-bearing alternative. A solid suit for a scavenger on a budget.\n \n
en.greh_armored_camo_outfit_description: A more advanced variant of regular Sin flak armor, typically gifted to the faction's more loyal and devout members. These suits do a great job fortifying the already durable warriors of Sin.\n \n
en.specops_dolg_outfit_description: This outfit comprises military PS5 series body armor with beryllium sputter modified for use in the Zone. Compared to a standard PS5-M universal protection suit, this outfit is more ergonomic and protective overall.\n \n
en.st_helm_respirator_descr: An ShMB gas mask refitted with external 40mm filters for greater functionality. As a general rule, masks like this offer respectable protection in areas of increased radioactivity but suffer from a high failure rate in toxic environments.\n \n
en.specops_outfit_description: This outfit comprises military PS5 series body armor with beryllium sputter modified for use in the Zone. Designed for intense rapid-insertion assault operations, this outfit is standard issue for most Spetsnaz operators in the Zone.\n \n
en.military_exo_outfit_description: The official military exoskeleton. Released in limited quantities by special order to Military stalkers who paid for it themselves. Designed to carry heavy weapons, along with enough ammunition, food and meds to keep the unit supplied for long periods of time. Occasionally fielded in lieu of light vehicles.\n \n
en.st_helm_cloth_mask_descr: A simple piece of cloth, folded into a mask. Stalkers will often drape it around their mouth and nose to avoid breathing in toxic or radioactive particles, but this protection is unreliable at best.\n \n
en.st_helm_resp_descr: A basic plastic and rubber respirator, used to protect the wearer from inhaling harmful dust, fumes, vapors or gases. Widely used by rookies due to its light weight and economical environmental protection. However, it provides negligible physical protection and is guaranteed to fail in more heavily irradiated areas.\n \n
en.dolg_proto_exo_outfit_description: A PS5-M universal protection bodysuit, specially designed to be fitted with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection but is also considerably lighter.\n \n
en.stalker_tigerstripe_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. One of the heavier stalker suits available, this outfit was designed for those who prefer to make their money as hired killers or fighters instead of looting artefacts or skinning mutants.\n \n
en.svoboda_heavy_outfit_2_description: An aged, slightly heavier stalker bodysuit made long ago by Freedom craftsmen. Although the suit has obviously acquired some wear and tear in places, it's roughly on par with most other basic stalker suits, even today. Younger Freedom members will pay through the nose to acquire one of these outfits, simply because of the amazing stories these venerable suits have acquired over the years.\n \n
en.trenchcoat_green_outfit_description: A long, thick green overcoat worn over a butchered scientist suit. Although the closed-cycle system and several other features seem to have been stripped off, the end result still compares favorably to many basic stalker suits.\n \n
en.app_tooltip_reliability: Higher reliability means less wear per shot, reducing the chance of a malfunction.
en.app_tooltip_malfunction: Chance of a malfunction on each shot. When one happens, a random part breaks and takes 5–12% damage regardless of weapon type or condition.
